expectations.text: Has subject line and preview text
expectations.evidence: All 3 variations have subject + preview. V1: 'Your invitation is ready — your guests are going to love it' / 'One click and the celebration begins.'

expectations.text: Acknowledges that event planning is busy / empathizes with delay
expectations.evidence: V2: 'Planning a celebration means juggling a lot of details. Venue, food, timing, guest list — we get it. Some things end up on the back burner.' V1: 'We know event planning can feel like a long to-do list.'

expectations.text: Reminds them their invitation is ready and waiting
expectations.evidence: All variations reference the week-old draft. V1: 'A week ago, you created something beautiful on InviteJoy. Your invitation is designed, saved, and ready to go.'

expectations.text: Makes publishing feel easy (one click, simple process)
expectations.evidence: V2: 'All that's left is one click.' V3: 'One click to publish. One link to share. That's the entire process.' Strongly emphasizes simplicity.

expectations.text: Clear CTA to publish the invitation
expectations.evidence: Consistent specific CTAs: '[Publish Your Invitation Now]', '[Publish and Share Your Invitation]', '[Publish Your Invitation]'. All action-specific.

expectations.text: Warm and supportive tone, not nagging
expectations.evidence: V1: 'this step? This one is the fun part.' V3: 'Cheering you on.' All P.S. lines address objections gently (editing, no rush). Never guilt-inducing.
3 variations: BAB (warm encouragement), PAS (supportive), PPPP (excitement-forward). Each with strategic notes, A/B testing, and global audience fit notes.
